Arcade.metaDescription: The ultimate summer hackathon for high schoolers. Make projects. Track your hours. Redeem for prizes.
Arcade.heroTagline: The summer is yours for the making
Arcade.heroNote: The Arcade closed September 1st, but you can still join the <slack>Hack Club Slack</slack>!
Arcade.step1Text: Hack on something cool. Examples: making your own PCB, building your own site, creating an app.
Arcade.step2Text: Join the <slack>Hack Club Slack</slack> and use /hack in #arcade to log your hours. You earn a ticket for each hour spent.
Arcade.step3Text: Use your tickets to buy prizes for your next project, like Arduino kits, drawing tablets, and more.
Arcade.stickerLine: Get <stickers>free stickers</stickers> and code with other high schoolers!
Arcade.buildWhateverBody: Any technical project counts. You could build an AR game, pixel art display, drawing robot, and more. Anytime you work on your project, start the hack hour timer. You earn a ticket for every hour.
Arcade.arcadeEndedThanks: Arcade has ended! Thanks for playing.
Arcade.prizesSubtitle: Redeem these with your tickets. For high schoolers or younger only.
Arcade.sneakPeek: This is just a <peek>sneak peek</peek>... there were many more prizes that were unlocked as the summer went on!

projectCards.elijah: Finally shipped my personal AI clone and had a ton of fun seeing what other people did with it.
projectCards.konstantinos: A drone build that can follow the biggest object of a chosen color.

starters.wizardDesc: Build a text-based game with AI.
starters.binDesc: Build an online circuit, get the parts for free.
starters.sprigDesc: Build a JS game, play it on your own console.
starters.onboardDesc: Design a PCB, get a $100 grant.
starters.hackaccinoDesc: Build a 3D website and get a free frappuccino.
starters.blotDesc: Write code. Make art. Get a drawing machine.
starters.ciderDesc: Make a mobile app and get an Apple Developer account.
starters.easelDesc: Write a programming language and receive fudge.

faq.eligibleA: You need to be a high schooler or younger. You just need a GitHub account and a Slack account to participate.
faq.projectsA: Projects need to be open source and have a way for people to experience them. Document progress as you go.
faq.howManyQ: How many projects can I build?
faq.howManyA: You can submit as many projects as you make. We just count the hours.
faq.costA: It is free. Some shipments may have customs charges depending on your country.
faq.helpA: Get help in the #arcade-help channel in the Hack Club Slack.
faq.hoursA: Reviews are done by humans and may take a few days to show up.
faq.teamA: Yes, but your own work needs to be clearly documented.
faq.schoolQ: What about school work or a job?
faq.schoolA: If you are building for school or work, it does not count for Arcade.
faq.scrapA: Code needs a commit. Share links for projects like Sprig, Blot, or hosted 3D models.
